reason: Both answers correctly state the §412 rule, correctly note registration is required before suing (Fourth Estate), correctly identify actual damages/injunction/DMCA as available fallbacks, and correctly note new post-registration infringement could still qualify for statutory damages. Both compute that the mid-January publication's 3-month grace window closed around mid-April, before today. The key difference: A states as settled fact that courts treat ongoing infringement as a single act commencing at first infringement, precluding statutory damages for later continuation, while B correctly flags this as a genuine circuit split and hedges appropriately. A is somewhat more useful practically (registration costs, expedited processing option, §504(b) profits). These trade-offs roughly offset.
